source_node: Jefferson Medical College Department of Surgery
notes: Chevalier Quixote Jackson (1865-1958) enrolled at Jefferson Medical College in 1884 and received his MD in 1886. He later returned to Jefferson as Professor of Laryngology (1916-1924) and Professor of Bronchoesophagology and department head (1924-1930). Eriksson et al (Surg Endosc 2023, PMID 37439820) and the Jefferson Libraries archives confirm his MD and professorial appointments. Jackson was a founding member and inaugural governor of the American College of Surgeons, the father of endoscopic surgery, and a pioneer in public health advocacy (Federal Caustic Poison Act of 1927). BRIDGE EDGE: Merges the Jackson/ABEA island (Component 6, 2 nodes) into the main component via the Jefferson Medical College node in 13_pre_halsted. Components: 8 to 7 (accounting for T1-03 bridge already reducing to 8). TARGET MODULE: 08_subspecialties.json

source_node: American Board of Otolaryngology-Head and Neck Surgery
notes: The American Board of Otolaryngology (ABOto) was founded and incorporated in 1924, the second specialty certifying body established after the American Board of Ophthalmology (1916). At the initiative of the Triological Society in 1912, the proposal for standardized post-graduate certification was published in The Laryngoscope in 1913. The ABOHNS is a founding member board of ABMS (established 1933) and confirmed as one of 24 current ABMS member boards on the ABMS website. This edge follows the identical pattern of existing board-ABMS edges (ABS, ABOS, ABNS, ABCRS, ABPS). BRIDGE EDGE: Merges the ABO-HNS/Triological island (Component 7, 2 nodes) into the main component via the ABMS governance backbone. TARGET MODULE: 12_governance_societies.json

source_node: University of Michigan Department of Surgery
notes: Norman Winslow Thompson spent his entire academic career at the University of Michigan, where he trained and rose to become the Henry King Ransom Professor of Surgery. He established the Division of Endocrine Surgery in 1979 (the first of its kind in the US), created the first comprehensive endocrine surgery fellowship in 1984, and co-founded the AAES in 1979 (serving as inaugural president, 1980). Thompson (Surgery 2011, PMID 22136854) authored the account of the AAES founding. The ASA memorial confirms his entire career was at Michigan. Exact residency dates approximated. Confidence moderate due to estimated training dates; the institutional relationship itself is unambiguous. BRIDGE EDGE: Merges the AAES/Thompson island (Component 8, 2 nodes) into the main component via the University of Michigan node (Coller's department in 02_general_surgery_spread). TARGET MODULE: 08_subspecialties.json

The American Society of Breast Surgeons (ASBrS) is formally affiliated with the American College of Surgeons. ASBrS members are required to be Fellows of the ACS or eligible for ACS fellowship. The ASBrS participates in the ACS National Accreditation Program for Breast Centers (NAPBC), established 2007, which sets standards for breast center accreditation. The ASBrS also collaborates with the ACS Commission on Cancer on breast surgery quality measures. Confidence moderate because the relationship is primarily through the NAPBC accreditation program rather than a direct governance hierarchy. BRIDGE EDGE: Merges the ASBrS/Zannis island (Component 4, 2 nodes) into the main component via the ACS governance hub. TARGET MODULE: 07_oncology_trials.json

source_node: St. Mark's Hospital for Fistula and Other Diseases of the Rectum
target_node: American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons
notes: St. Mark's Hospital and the ASCRS have maintained a formal institutional relationship through the Tripartite Colorectal Meeting, a rotating international conference jointly organized by the ASCRS, St. Mark's (via the Association of Coloproctology of Great Britain and Ireland, ACPGBI), and the Colorectal Surgical Society of Australia and New Zealand. The first Tripartite meeting was held in 1993. Perry et al (Colorectal Dis 2021, PMID 33421312) document 30 years of the ACPGBI including the Tripartite relationship with ASCRS. St. Mark's surgeons have been regular visiting professors at ASCRS and vice versa, and the institutions share fellowship exchange programs. This is an observational_study edge representing sustained institutional knowledge exchange rather than a formal accreditation or governance relationship. Confidence moderate because the relationship is bilateral and collegial rather than hierarchical. BRIDGE EDGE: Merges the Salmon/St. Mark's island (Component 5, 2 nodes) into the main component via the ASCRS node in 08_subspecialties. TARGET MODULE: 08_subspecialties.json
